signal.raw_content:
Pharmaceutical R&D Productivity: Trends and Challenges — McKinsey Life Sciences 2026 Report

McKinsey & Company today published its annual analysis of pharmaceutical R&D productivity, examining clinical success rates, time-to-approval, cost-per-approved-molecule, and industry investment trends.

KEY FINDINGS:

Phase 2 Success Rates: Industry-wide Phase 2 clinical success rates have improved modestly to 38% in 2025, up from 34% in 2020. Oncology remains the most active therapeutic area by investment but continues to have below-average success rates (29% Phase 2 to Phase 3 transition).

Cost Per Approved Drug: Fully-loaded R&D cost per approved drug (including capital costs and failures) has reached $2.9 billion per approved molecule, up from $2.6 billion in 2022. Rising clinical trial costs, patient recruitment challenges, and post-marketing commitment requirements are primary drivers.

AI in Drug Discovery: 47% of surveyed companies report using generative AI tools for molecular design and hit identification, up from 11% in 2022. However, McKinsey cautions that AI-generated candidates have not yet demonstrated improved Phase 2 success rates compared to traditional approaches.

Pipeline Concentration: The top 20 pharmaceutical companies account for 61% of global R&D spend but only 42% of pipeline candidates by count, reflecting the growing role of biotech in early-stage innovation.

This report focuses on R&D productivity metrics and drug development strategy. It does not address pharmaceutical manufacturing operations, API synthesis, or supply chain risk.
